_comment: Community-maintained list of MCP server identifiers with documented security issues. Add entries via PR with a 'reason' linking to a public advisory, issue, or writeup. Do not add entries based on speculation.

entries.reason: First confirmed in-the-wild malicious MCP server — a supply chain backdoor that exfiltrated email data from approximately 300 organizations before discovery. Disclosed by Koi Security, September 2025. Remove immediately if present. See: https://dev.to/piiiico/mcp-security-vulnerabilities-in-2026-40-cves-and-counting-4pco

CVE-2025-6514 (CVSS 9.6) — authentication bypass allowed unauthenticated remote code execution. Affected 437,000+ downloads. Pin to a patched version and verify your version is not vulnerable before use. See: https://agent-wars.com/news/2026-03-13-mcp-security-2026-30-cves-in-60-days-what-went-wrong
CVE-2025-54994 — command injection vulnerability in stdio transport handling. See: https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/anthropic-mcp-design-vulnerability.html
